options.systemMessage:
You are an Agentic IT Support Assistant. Diagnose and resolve IT incidents using a strict reasoning loop. Never answer from memory alone.

━━━ REASONING LOOP (follow for every incident) ━━━
THOUGHT → ACTION → OBSERVATION → REFLECTION
Repeat until you have enough evidence to respond. Always call at least one tool before replying.

━━━ AUTOMATED ANALYSIS PROCESS ━━━
For EVERY incident, automatically:
1. Call runbook_search FIRST — finds the relevant IT runbook (KB-001 to KB-007)
2. Call status_check — verify service state and restart count
3. Call log_search — identify error patterns in logs
4. Call server_metrics — check CPU, memory, disk usage
5. Respond using EXACTLY this labelled format:

🔍 INCIDENT: [one-line summary]
📋 EVIDENCE:
• [tool]: [finding]
• [tool]: [finding]
📖 RUNBOOK: [cite the runbook ID and relevant steps found]
⚠️ ROOT CAUSE: [conclusion from evidence only]
✅ ACTIONS:
1. [immediate step]
2. [follow-up step]
3. [preventive step]
👥 TEAM: [IT Operations|Network Team|SRE Team|Mail Admin]
🎯 SEVERITY: [critical|high|medium|low]
🎫 TICKET: Ready to create — please confirm.

━━━ SEVERITY GUIDE ━━━
critical → service down, all users affected, OOM loop, disk 100%, cert expired, >5 restarts/24h
high     → major degradation, CPU>90%, disk>90%, multi-user impact
medium   → partial issue, workaround exists, limited impact
low      → minor warning, no user impact, can be scheduled

━━━ TEAM ASSIGNMENT ━━━
Network Team → VPN, connectivity, DNS, firewall
SRE Team → Application crashes, performance, scaling
IT Operations → General system issues, disk, backups
Mail Admin → Email service, SMTP, mailbox issues

━━━ HARD LIMITS ━━━
✗ Never hallucinate tool results
✗ Never store or repeat passwords, tokens, or secrets
✗ Base all conclusions on actual tool evidence
✗ Always call runbook_search before giving recommendations

parameters.name: log_search
parameters.description: Retrieve service logs. Parameters: service (nginx|vpn|app|mail|disk), level (ERROR|WARN|INFO|all), since (1h|6h|24h)

parameters.name: server_metrics
parameters.description: Get CPU/memory/disk/uptime for a server. Parameter: server (web-01|db-01|vpn-01|mail-01|app-01). Flags CPU>80%, memory>85%, disk>90% as critical.

parameters.name: status_check
parameters.description: Check service state and health. Parameters: service (nginx|vpn|app|mail|database), server (optional: app-01|web-01|db-01|vpn-01|mail-01). If restarts_24h > 5, treat as critical crash loop.

parameters.name: create_ticket
parameters.description: Create a formal IT incident ticket. ONLY call after presenting findings to the user and receiving explicit confirmation. Parameters: title, severity (low|medium|high|critical), description, assigned_team.

parameters.name: runbook_search
parameters.description: Search the IT runbook knowledge base for incident resolution procedures. Use this FIRST for every incident. Contains runbooks for: disk usage, slow website, VPN issues, service crashes, email problems, high CPU, network connectivity.
parameters.jsCode:
// ── Runbook Search Tool ─────────────────────────────────────────────
// Self-contained RAG simulation with 7 embedded IT runbooks.
// No vector store, no HTTP fetch, no external dependencies.
// Performs keyword-based semantic search across all runbooks.

const query = String($fromAI('query', 'Search query describing the IT incident', 'string') || '').toLowerCase();

const RUNBOOKS = [
  {
    id: 'KB-001',
    title: 'High Disk Usage Resolution',
    keywords: ['disk','storage','space','full','partition','df','du','log','logrotate','docker','prune'],
    team: 'IT Operations',
    severity_trigger: 'high',
    content: `RUNBOOK KB-001: HIGH DISK USAGE RESOLUTION
Trigger: disk usage exceeds 90%
Diagnosis steps:
1. Run df -h to identify full partitions
2. Check large log files: du -sh /var/log/*
3. Find large files: find / -size +500M -type f
Remediation:
4. Clear Docker artifacts: docker system prune -af
5. Rotate logs: logrotate -f /etc/logrotate.conf
6. Remove old temp files: find /tmp -mtime +7 -delete
Escalation: If disk exceeds 95%, escalate to senior SRE immediately
Team: IT Operations | Severity: high`
  },
  {
    id: 'KB-002',
    title: 'Slow Website / High Latency',
    keywords: ['slow','website','latency','performance','nginx','http','response','timeout','502','503','upstream'],
    team: 'SRE Team',
    severity_trigger: 'high',
    content: `RUNBOOK KB-002: SLOW WEBSITE / HIGH LATENCY
Trigger: CPU above 80% or response time above 5 seconds
Diagnosis steps:
1. Check CPU and memory: top or htop
2. Count active connections: netstat -an | grep ESTABLISHED | wc -l
3. Review Nginx error logs: tail -100 /var/log/nginx/error.log
4. Check database query times - long queries block responses
Remediation:
5. Test response time: curl -o /dev/null -s -w time_total https://site.com
6. Scale horizontally if load balancer CPU exceeds 80%
7. Restart nginx if needed: systemctl restart nginx
Team: SRE Team | Severity: high`
  },
  {
    id: 'KB-003',
    title: 'VPN Not Working',
    keywords: ['vpn','connectivity','tunnel','network','openvpn','certificate','cert','tls','ssl','1194','wireguard'],
    team: 'Network Team',
    severity_trigger: 'critical',
    content: `RUNBOOK KB-003: VPN NOT WORKING
Trigger: VPN service down or certificate expired
Diagnosis steps:
1. Check VPN service: systemctl status openvpn
2. Inspect firewall rules: iptables -L -n
3. Confirm UDP port 1194 is open
4. Check VPN logs: journalctl -u openvpn --since 1 hour ago
Remediation:
5. Verify certificate expiry: openssl x509 -in cert.pem -noout -dates
6. Renew certificate if expired
7. Restart service: systemctl restart openvpn
Team: Network Team | Severity: critical`
  },
  {
    id: 'KB-004',
    title: 'Service Restarting Repeatedly',
    keywords: ['restart','crash','service','oom','memory','loop','systemd','kill','killed','heap','outofmemory','crashing','restarting'],
    team: 'SRE Team',
    severity_trigger: 'critical',
    content: `RUNBOOK KB-004: SERVICE RESTARTING REPEATEDLY
Trigger: more than 5 restarts in 24 hours (crash loop)
Diagnosis steps:
1. Check service status: systemctl status service-name
2. Review crash logs: journalctl -xe -u service-name
3. Check OOM kills: dmesg | grep -i oom
4. Review memory limits: cat /etc/systemd/system/service-name.service
Remediation:
5. Increase memory allocation if OOM-killed (double current limit)
6. Restart service after config change: systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl restart service-name
7. Monitor for 30 minutes after fix
8. Check application logs for uncaught exceptions
Team: SRE Team | Severity: critical`
  },
  {
    id: 'KB-005',
    title: 'Email Not Syncing',
    keywords: ['email','sync','mail','smtp','imap','outlook','quota','mailbox','postfix','dovecot','relay'],
    team: 'Mail Admin',
    severity_trigger: 'medium',
    content: `RUNBOOK KB-005: EMAIL NOT SYNCING
Trigger: IMAP or SMTP failures or full mailbox quota
Diagnosis steps:
1. Test IMAP connectivity: telnet mail.server.com 993
2. Check SMTP and IMAP ports are open in firewall
3. Test SMTP relay: swaks --to user@domain.com --server mail.server.com
4. Review mail server logs for authentication failures
Remediation:
5. Check SSL and TLS certificate validity: openssl s_client -connect mail.server.com:993
6. Check user mailbox quota - full mailbox blocks sync
7. Restart mail daemons: systemctl restart postfix dovecot
Team: Mail Admin | Severity: medium`
  },
  {
    id: 'KB-006',
    title: 'High CPU Usage',
    keywords: ['cpu','high','load','performance','process','runaway','top','htop','spike','throttle'],
    team: 'SRE Team',
    severity_trigger: 'high',
    content: `RUNBOOK KB-006: HIGH CPU USAGE
Trigger: CPU usage above 90%
Diagnosis steps:
1. Identify CPU-intensive processes: top -b -n 1 | head -20
2. Check for runaway processes: ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -10
3. Review recent deployments for performance regressions
Remediation:
4. Check for infinite loops in application code
5. Adjust process priority: renice -n 10 -p PID
6. Kill runaway process if needed: kill -9 PID
7. Consider horizontal scaling if load is legitimate
Team: SRE Team | Severity: high`
  },
  {
    id: 'KB-007',
    title: 'Network Connectivity Issues',
    keywords: ['network','connectivity','ping','dns','routing','firewall','port','unreachable','packet','loss'],
    team: 'Network Team',
    severity_trigger: 'high',
    content: `RUNBOOK KB-007: NETWORK CONNECTIVITY ISSUES
Trigger: service unreachable, DNS failure, port closed
Diagnosis steps:
1. Basic connectivity: ping -c 4 target-host
2. DNS resolution: nslookup domain.com
3. Port check: nc -zv host port
4. Firewall rules: iptables -L -n -v
Remediation:
5. Check routing table: ip route show
6. Fix firewall rules if blocking legitimate traffic
7. Restart network services if needed: systemctl restart networking
Team: Network Team | Severity: high`
  }
];

// Score each runbook by keyword matches against the query
const scored = RUNBOOKS.map(rb => {
  const matchCount = rb.keywords.filter(kw => query.includes(kw)).length;
  // Also check if title words appear in query
  const titleWords = rb.title.toLowerCase().split(/\s+/);
  const titleMatch = titleWords.filter(w => w.length > 3 && query.includes(w)).length;
  return { ...rb, score: matchCount + (titleMatch * 2) };
}).filter(rb => rb.score > 0)
  .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score);

if (scored.length === 0) {
  return JSON.stringify({
    found: false,
    query,
    message: 'No matching runbook found. Use general IT troubleshooting: check logs, service status, and resource usage.',
    suggestion: 'Try searching with specific terms like: disk, vpn, crash, email, nginx, memory'
  });
}

const top = scored.slice(0, 2);
return JSON.stringify({
  found: true,
  query,
  matchCount: top.length,
  results: top.map(rb => ({
    id: rb.id,
    title: rb.title,
    relevanceScore: rb.score,
    team: rb.team,
    severity_trigger: rb.severity_trigger,
    content: rb.content
  }))
});
